description: Lock Certificate Transparency to RFC 6962's 2013 X.509 logging protocol rather than backdating it to earlier digital-identity systems.

checks.reason: RFC 6962 logs X.509 certificate chains and evaluates them against roots accepted by each log.
Certificate issuance, chains, and trust roots are the protocol objects being made transparent.
The first Certificate Transparency specification, RFC 6962, was published in June 2013.
